content.text:
Instructions: Answer the questions using proper grammar and vocabulary and relate your response to a real-life example. Please provide as much detail as you can.
答題指示：請使用適當的文法和詞彙，並結合現實生活的情境回答問題。請儘可能詳細回答。

Explain the word “manager”
解釋「經理」這個詞
content.assessment_standard:
10	A manager is a person who leads a team and ensures that work is completed efficiently. They are responsible for planning, organizing, and making important decisions. Managers also communicate with employees to solve problems and improve productivity. Their leadership is essential for a company’s success.|A manager is someone who takes charge of a group and makes sure tasks are completed properly. For example, in a restaurant, a manager supervises staff, handles customer complaints, and ensures smooth operations. Good managers also motivate their team to provide excellent service.|A sports manager is responsible for organizing a team and planning events. They arrange training sessions, communicate with coaches and players, and handle sponsorships. A manager also ensures that everything runs smoothly during competitions. Their work helps teams stay well-prepared and successful.
9	A manager leads a company’s team and makes important decisions. They organize work, assign tasks, and ensure employees complete their duties. Managers also solve problems and help the company operate efficiently.|A manager takes care of a group’s work and makes sure everything is done well. For example, in a school club, a manager plans activities and keeps the team organized. They also help solve problems and guide the group.|A sports manager helps organize a team and plan events. They arrange practice schedules, communicate with players, and handle contracts. Their job is important for keeping the team running smoothly.
8	A sports manager helps organize a team and plan events. They arrange practice schedules, communicate with players, and handle contracts. Their job is important for keeping the team running smoothly.|A manager is someone who helps a group do their work. In a store, a manager tells workers what to do and checks their progress. They also help solve problems when needed.|A manager in sports takes care of the team. They plan practice and make sure everything is ready for games. Managers also help with travel and events.
7	A manager tells workers their jobs and checks their work. They make plans and fix problems in a company.|A manager helps a group work together. They give instructions and make sure things are done.|A sports manager helps plan games and take care of players. They make sure everything is ready.
6	A manager is a person who tells people what to do in a company. They check work and solve some problems.|A manager gives jobs to people and makes sure they finish. They help when there is a problem.|A manager helps sports teams by planning and making schedules. They help teams play well.
5	A manager is the boss in a company. They tell workers what to do.|A manager tells team members what to do and makes sure they finish.|A manager plans sports games and helps teams.
4	A manager is the leader. They take care of work.|A manager helps a group do something.|A manager plans games and helps players.
3	Manager is work. Company boss. Job control.|Manager tells people. Team work.|Sports manager. Plan. Team.
2	Boss. Work. Job.|People. Leader. Task.|Game. Coach. Organize.
1	(No response / Silence)

Instructions: Answer the questions using proper grammar and vocabulary and relate your response to a real-life example. Please provide as much detail as you can.

Explain the word “manager”
解釋「經理」這個詞
content.assessment_standard:
10	I really enjoy hiking because it allows me to connect with nature and escape from daily stress. The fresh air and beautiful scenery make me feel refreshed and energized. Additionally, hiking is a great way to exercise and stay fit while exploring new places. Last summer, I hiked a mountain trail with my friends, and the view from the top was absolutely breathtaking.|Although hiking can be a good workout, I don’t particularly enjoy it because it can be physically exhausting. I prefer activities that require less endurance, such as swimming or cycling. Also, hiking trails can sometimes be dangerous, especially in bad weather. One time, I tried hiking in the rain, and I slipped multiple times, which made the experience unpleasant.|Hiking has both enjoyable and challenging aspects for me. On one hand, I love the peacefulness of being in nature and seeing new landscapes. On the other hand, I don’t like long and difficult hikes because they make me too tired. If the trail is short and not too steep, I enjoy it. However, if it’s a long and tough hike, I would rather stay home and relax.
9	I like hiking because it is fun and helps me stay healthy. Walking in nature makes me feel relaxed, and I enjoy seeing different plants and animals. I also like hiking with friends because we can talk and take pictures together.|I don’t really enjoy hiking because it is too tiring. I prefer doing indoor activities, like reading or watching movies. Also, hiking takes a lot of time, and sometimes the weather is too hot or cold, which makes it uncomfortable.|Hiking is fun sometimes, but not always. I like it when the trail is easy and the weather is nice. However, if the trail is too long or difficult, I don’t enjoy it. I also don’t like hiking alone because it feels boring.
8	I enjoy hiking because it is good exercise. It also lets me see beautiful places like mountains and rivers. I like going with friends because we can have fun together.|I don’t like hiking because it makes me tired. I prefer staying home and doing other things. Also, sometimes the weather is too hot, and that makes hiking uncomfortable.|I like hiking sometimes, but only if the trail is easy. If it is too hard, I don’t enjoy it. I also don’t like hiking alone, but with friends, it is fun.
7	I like hiking because it is fun and good for my body. I like to see trees and animals.|I don’t like hiking. It is too hard for me. I get tired very fast.|Hiking is okay. Sometimes I like it, but sometimes it is too hard.
6	Hiking is good. I like to walk outside.|I don’t like hiking. It is too hard.|Sometimes hiking is good, but not always.
5	I like hiking. It is fun.|I don’t like hiking. It is hard.|Hiking is okay. Sometimes fun.
4	Hiking fun.|No hiking. Tired.|Hiking, good or bad.
3	Hiking good.|No like hiking.|Hiking… maybe good.
2	Good.|Bad.|Maybe.
1	(No response / Silence)

4	The total cost for a Beef Wellington and a Chocolate Cake is $34.|Ordering a Beef Wellington and a Chocolate Cake costs $34 in total.|If you order a Beef Wellington and a Chocolate Cake, the total price is $34.

content.text:
Instructions: Answer the questions using proper grammar and vocabulary and relate your response to a real-life example. Please provide as much detail as you can.
答題指示：請使用適當的文法和詞彙，並結合現實生活的情境回答問題。請儘可能詳細回答。

Explain the word “manager”
解釋「經理」這個詞
content.assessment_standard:
10	I believe schools should require students to wear uniforms because it promotes equality among students. When everyone wears the same clothes, there is less pressure to follow fashion trends, which can reduce bullying based on appearance. Additionally, uniforms help students focus on their studies rather than worrying about what to wear. In my experience, wearing a uniform also saves time in the morning since I don’t have to decide on an outfit.|I don’t think schools should require students to wear uniforms because it limits self-expression. Clothing is a way for students to show their personality, and forcing them to wear the same outfit takes away their individuality. Moreover, some uniforms can be uncomfortable, especially in hot weather. I remember wearing a school uniform that was made of thick fabric, and it made me feel too warm during summer.|There are both advantages and disadvantages to school uniforms. On one hand, they create a sense of unity and help students look professional. On the other hand, they can be restrictive and expensive for some families. A better solution might be to have a flexible dress code that allows students to choose from a few uniform options rather than requiring them to wear the exact same outfit.
9	I think students should wear uniforms because it makes everyone look equal. It also helps students focus on school instead of fashion. Wearing a uniform saves time in the morning, too.|I don’t agree with school uniforms because they stop students from showing their personality. Some uniforms are also uncomfortable to wear, especially in hot weather.|I don’t agree with school uniforms because they stop students from showing their personality. Some uniforms are also uncomfortable to wear, especially in hot weather.
8	I think uniforms are good because they make students look the same and reduce bullying. Also, they help students focus on learning.|I don’t like uniforms because they are not comfortable. Students should choose their own clothes to show their style.|Uniforms are helpful, but they are also strict. I think students should have some choice in what they wear.
7	Uniforms are good. They make students look nice and stop bullying.|I don’t like uniforms. They are hot and not comfortable.|Uniforms are okay. Good and bad.
6	Uniforms good. Same clothes, no problem.|I don’t like. Uniform hot.|Uniforms okay, but not always.
5	I like uniforms. They are good.|No uniform. Not good.|Maybe uniforms are good, maybe bad.
4	Uniforms are good. They help students look the same.|Uniforms are bad. No personal style.|Uniforms help students, but also not free.
3	Uniforms good. Same look.|No uniforms. Bad.|Uniforms maybe good, maybe bad. Not sure.
2	Good.|Bad.|Maybe.
1	(No response / Silence)

Instructions: Answer the questions using proper grammar and vocabulary and relate your response to a real-life example. Please provide as much detail as you can.

Explain the word “manager”
解釋「經理」這個詞
content.assessment_standard:
10	I prefer working in a café because the background noise helps me stay focused. The lively atmosphere makes me feel motivated and productive. Also, I enjoy drinking coffee while working, which keeps me energized. Last week, I worked in a café for three hours and managed to complete all my tasks efficiently.|I prefer working at home because it provides a quiet and comfortable environment. At home, I can set up my workspace the way I like, which helps me stay focused. Also, I don’t have to worry about finding a seat or spending money on coffee. I feel more relaxed when I work in my own space.|Both working in a café and at home have advantages. Cafés are great for a change of environment and have a lively atmosphere, but they can be noisy and distracting. On the other hand, working at home is comfortable and quiet, but sometimes it can feel isolating. I like switching between the two depending on my mood and the type of work I need to do.
9	I like working in a café because the noise helps me concentrate. Also, I can enjoy a nice drink while working, which makes me feel more productive.|I prefer working at home because it is quiet and comfortable. I don’t have to worry about noise or spending money at a café.|Sometimes I like working in a café because it’s a nice change, but I also enjoy working at home because it’s quieter.
8	Cafés are good for working because they have a nice atmosphere. I like the sound of people talking and coffee machines.|I like working at home because it is quiet. I can focus better and work without distractions.|Cafés are nice, but home is also comfortable. I think both are good depending on the situation.
7	I like working in a café. It is a nice place, and I can drink coffee.|I prefer home. It is quiet, and I can work alone.|Both café and home are okay. It depends on the work.
6	Cafés are good for work. They have coffee and music.|Home is better. No noise, so I can work well.|Cafés are good, but home is also good.
5	Café is good. I like coffee and working there.|Home is quiet. Good for work.|Café good, home good too.
4	Café good for work. Coffee nice.|Home better. Quiet, good work.|Café good, home also good.
3	Café good. Coffee nice.|Home quiet. Good.|Maybe café, maybe home.
2	Café.|Home.|Maybe.
1	(No response / Silence)
